pattern: The deepest point of a branching tree is exactly one more than whichever child subtree reaches furthest, with childless leaves anchoring the recursion at nothing extra.
hints: A leaf node (no children) contributes a depth of exactly one — there is nothing further beneath it.
For any node, its depth is one plus the largest depth among its children — an empty children list makes that added term zero.
Take the maximum over children at every node, never the sum, and remember an entirely empty tree has depth zero, not one.

edge_inputs.description: an empty tree has depth zero
a lone root has depth one
one root fanning out to nine children has depth two regardless of how many children exist at that level
a single-child chain six nodes deep has depth six, testing that width never gets confused with depth
an irregular tree where different branches bottom out at different depths, so the max must come from the deepest branch
repeated values across separate nodes so depth counting can't accidentally collapse distinct nodes that share a value

stress.description: random n-ary trees grown by attaching each new node to a random existing node with up to 4 children
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 80)))
    children = {1: []}
    order = [1]
    for v in range(2, n + 1):
        candidates = [p for p in order if len(children[p]) < 4]
        parent = rng.choice(candidates) if candidates else rng.choice(order)
        children[parent].append(v)
        children[v] = []
        order.append(v)
    wire = [1, None]
    queue = [1]
    while queue:
        node = queue.pop(0)
        for k in children[node]:
            wire.append(k)
            queue.append(k)
        wire.append(None)
    while wire and wire[-1] is None:
        wire.pop()
    return [wire]
